seed.note: twenty deliberately unordered reference records with distinct string, numeric, timestamp, secondary, and null values, seeded by the harness into an isolated reference resource

steps: seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted ascending by the string field
assert every adjacent record pair is in ascending string order
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted descending by the string field
assert every adjacent record pair is in descending string order
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted ascending by the numeric field
assert numeric order so 9 precedes 100 and never lexicographic; every adjacent pair ascending
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted descending by the numeric field
assert numeric order so 100 precedes 9 and never lexicographic; every adjacent pair descending
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted ascending by the timestamp field
assert every adjacent record pair is in ascending chronological order
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted descending by the timestamp field
assert every adjacent record pair is in descending chronological order
seed twenty records with repeated primary keys and distinct secondary values
GET the collection sorted by the primary field then the secondary field
assert records with equal primary keys keep their secondary order (stability assertion)
seed records including some with a null nullable-field value
GET the collection sorted by the nullable field
assert null values are placed per the documented nulls-first or nulls-last policy
seed records with mixed-case string values
GET the collection sorted by the string field
assert ordering follows the documented collation/case-sensitivity policy
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET each page of the sorted collection using the page-size role across page boundaries
assert ordering is stable and correct across page boundaries with no duplicate or dropped record
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection sorted by a field the contract does not list
assert the request is rejected with a 400
seed twenty unordered reference records
GET the collection with an order value that is neither asc nor desc
assert the request is rejected with a 400
